mentions.note: Core customer financially constrained; reduced SNAP benefits and higher fuel costs cutting visits; trip consolidation reducing shopping frequency; traditional weak-economy play broken.
Reports Tuesday; expect similar blowout to Dollar Tree which beat yesterday despite consumer weighed by fuel costs and inflation—resilience signal.
Worst S&P performer today despite being classic defensive/economically sensitive proxy; should be bought in weakness, not sold.
Value retailer peer; +4.3% SSS growth; cleaner execution vs. OLLI but not as aggressive growth.
Down 2.6%; should see positive lift if consumer trading down to dollar stores, but weakness signals deeper consumer pain than expected.

New CEO Jerry Fleman (experienced grocery executive) tasked with expanding food offerings. 20K+ stores with 450 new US stores planned plus 10 Mexico stores. Right environment for value retail. Caller Robert confident; Cramer agrees—appropriate business for this economic moment.
Off game currently, but historically resilient—don't bet against; likely to pull bull out of hat as it usually does.
Discount retailer reports tomorrow; Cramer endorses for trade-down theme if oil stabilizes and inflation persists.
Bargain play; real value proposition; benefits if oil doesn't spike to $120+; fits description of working stocks.
Value retailer with transparent pricing and understandable model; benefiting from rotation to companies making/selling things people want at lower cost.
Up 98% YTD with Todd Bosos as CEO; operational improvements from store standards to supply chain; Cramer visited store and confirmed excellent execution.
Dollar stores and discounters experiencing euphoric retail jailbreak on lower inflation; buyers can't find exit.
